evals.prompt: I need to create a skill for my project that uses Prisma with PostgreSQL. The devs keep getting confused by many-to-many relationships and nested includes.
evals.expected_output: A skill with a SKILL.md covering Prisma patterns: relationships, includes, queries, and naming conventions. Include evals and assertions.
assertions: Generates a SKILL.md with valid frontmatter (name, description)
The description uses the imperative phrase 'Use this skill when...'
Includes a 'When to use this skill' section
Includes rules with Correct/Incorrect examples for many-to-many relationships
Generates evals/evals.json with at least 3 test cases

evals.prompt: I want to create a skill for the components in my project. We have our own design system with tokens and naming conventions.
evals.expected_output: A skill that captures the design system: tokens, naming conventions, and component patterns. It explores the codebase first to extract real patterns.
assertions: Explores the codebase before creating the skill (looks for existing components)
Extracts real project patterns, not generic ones
The description covers synonyms (UI, components, design system, tokens)
Includes a directory structure if the design system is complex

evals.prompt: How do I write a good README for my project?
evals.expected_output: This should NOT trigger the skill-creator. Writing a README is not creating a skill.
assertions: The skill does NOT activate for this request
